column4: NIST archived SRM 2728 Certificate of Analysis title nominal composition and intended-use transcription
column5: The certificate title states nominal amount fractions of 14 mole percent carbon dioxide, 8 mole percent carbon monoxide, and 3000 micromoles per mole propane in nitrogen. The generic archived certificate leaves the cylinder-specific certified values and their 95 percent confidence intervals blank, so these rows preserve nominal title values with Unstated uncertainty and are not certified values for a physical cylinder. Nitrogen is identified only as the balance gas and is not assigned a derived scalar because the certificate also reports an estimated 8 micromoles per mole total-other-hydrocarbons information value. This is a calibration gas for mobile-source emissions inspection and maintenance, not a sampled or equilibrium engine exhaust state. Cylinder and sample identity, use temperature and pressure, oxygen, water, NOx, thermophysical properties, reactions, and transport data are absent
